source_pr.title: refactor update/insert/delete qualified name and as name handling
source_pr.body:
- Fix issue #369 
- Unify qualified and as handling for update/insert/delete.  
- When use as for a table, we can't use the origin table name.

symptoms: testSQL = `SELECT addresses.id, addresses.user_id, addresses.email_address FROM addresses ORDER BY addresses.id;` (stmt/stmts/update_test.go)
failure_signature: testSQL = `SELECT addresses.id, addresses.user_id, addresses.email_address FROM addresses ORDER BY addresses.id;` (stmt/stmts/update_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: testSQL = `SELECT addresses.id, addresses.user_id, addresses.email_address FROM addresses ORDER BY addresses.id;` (stmt/stmts/update_test.go)
files: plan/plans/join.go, stmt/stmts/delete.go, stmt/stmts/delete_test.go, stmt/stmts/insert.go, stmt/stmts/insert_test.go, stmt/stmts/update.go, stmt/stmts/update_test.go
